comment: Published lyrics by Ira Gershwin. Verse (Simply) + Refrain (well marked). Pipeline has stacked-verse garble in m42 (5L/4n). Gaps at m8 (1n, instrumental), m28 (1n), m31 (2n), m63 (2n, instrumental). The gaps at m28-m31 caused a lyric shift through m32-m39; pipeline notes were also undercounted in that range so a full lyric reconstruction is not possible -- best-effort corrections provided. From m40 onward the pipeline is back in sync.

9.comment: Pipeline had 'When I'm ing' (3L/3n). Dropped 'danc'. Published: 'When I'm danc-'.

18.comment: Pipeline had 'ban ia,.' (2L/2n). Spurious comma. Published: 'ban-ia.'.

19.comment: Pipeline had 'set I'm not up' (4L/4n). Word order garbled. Published: 'I'm not up-set,'.

28.comment: GAP: 1 note + 2 rests, no lyrics. Published: 'In' (pickup to 'In March or May').

29.comment: GAP: 2 notes + 1 rest, no lyrics. Published: 'March or'.

30.comment: GAP: 4 notes, no lyrics. Published: 'May, I can't be'.

31.comment: GAP: 2 notes + 2 rests, no lyrics. Published: 'both-ered'.

32.comment: Pipeline had 'and' (1L/1n). Shifted from gap. Published: 'now!' (completing 'I can't be both-ered now!').

33.comment: Pipeline had 'news' (1L/1n). Shifted. Published: pipeline lost notes in this range; best-effort: 'My' (start of 'My bonds and shares').

37.comment: Pipeline had 'cares? round I'm' (3L/3n). Shifted. Published: 'May fall down' (from 'May fall down-stairs,').

38.comment: Pipeline had 'day ing and I' (4L/4n). Shifted. Published: best-effort 'stairs, Who cares? who' (pipeline lost notes mean some syllables of 'who cares? I'm danc-ing and I can't be both-ered' cannot be placed).

39.comment: Pipeline had 'can't be both ered' (4L/4n). Shifted. Published: 'cares? I'm danc ing'.

42.comment: STACKED 5L/4n. Pipeline had 'up a mong the I'. Published v1 (removing v2 bleed 'I'): 'up a mong the'.

43.comment: Pipeline had 'stairs On' (2L/3n). 'stairs' is OCR for 'stars,'. Published: 'stars, On' -- only 2 syllables on 3 notes (one note may be tied or pickup).

59.comment: Pipeline had 'be both ereu' (3L/4n). 'ereu' is OCR garble for 'ered'. Published: 'be both-ered' -- 3 syllables on 4 notes (last note may be held).

62.comment: Pipeline had 'now' (1L/1n). Second ending. Published: 'now.' (with period, not bare).
